name: RESEARCH FRONTIERS INC
business: Research Frontiers develops and licenses its patented Suspended Particle Device (SPD) light-control technology to other companies that manufacture and/or market electronically dimmable glazing products for automotive, aerospace, architectural, marine, rail, recreational vehicle and specialty vehicle applications. The company generates revenue primarily through license fees and royalties paid by licensees and does not manufacture finished glazing products directly.

exposures.sentence: The Company currently believes that the automotive market will be the largest source of its royalty income over the next several years.
SPD-Smart electronically dimmable windows are currently flying on more than 40 aircraft models across commercial, business and specialty aviation segments.

SPD-Smart architectural products instantly and precisely provide shading, glare control and heat management solutions for offices and homes, especially when these products are available for new construction, replacement and retrofit projects.
SPD-Smart marine products provide an innovation that allows these operators to manage incoming light, glare and heat while achieving privacy or maintaining one's view as desired.

catalysts.event: Serial production and customer deliveries of SPD-SmartGlass on Cadillac CELESTIQ
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Gauzy and Research Frontiers announced that SPD-SmartGlass is in serial production and customer deliveries on General Motors Cadillac CELESTIQ electric sedan, featuring a multi-zone electronically dimmable roof - believed by the Company to be among the largest SPD-SmartGlass deployments in a production vehicle to date.

catalysts.event: Ferrari adoption of SPD-SmartGlass in Purosangue roof
catalysts.sentence: Ferrari adopted SPD-SmartGlass in the roof of its new Purosangue model.

catalysts.event: Introduction of prefabricated SPD-SmartGlass retrofit system for architectural market
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025 at GlassBuild America, Research Frontiers and its partners introduced a prefabricated SPD-SmartGlass retrofit system designed to enable conversion of existing building glazing without full window replacement.

tamClaims.claim: Global smart glass market expected to grow from $6.4 billion in 2025 to $10.4 billion by 2030 (CAGR 10.2%)
tamClaims.sentence: In January 2025, MarketsandMarkets issued Smart Glass Market by Technology (Suspended Particle Display (SPD), Electrochromic Glass, Liquid Crystal/PDLC, Photochromic, Thermochromic and other technologies), Application, Control Mode and Geographic Analysis Global Forecast to 2030. This market research report concludes that the global smart glass market is expected to grow from approximately USD $6.4 billion in 2025 to approximately USD $10.4 billion by 2030, representing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 10.2% between 2025 and 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Another report projects smart glass market to reach $13.0 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: Another recent industry report projects the smart glass market to reach approximately USD $13.0 billion by 2030, reflecting a compound annual growth rate in the high single-digit to low double-digit range during the forecast period.

tamClaims.claim: Long-term forecast suggests smart glass market could exceed $18 billion by mid-2030s
tamClaims.sentence: Longer-term forecasts based upon third-party research estimates suggest that the global smart glass market could exceed USD $18 billion by the mid-2030s, depending on forecast methodology and segmentation assumptions.
